human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for accounting-service analytics specificity, close and reconciliation metric rigor, workpaper support, confidentiality handling, advisory and attestation boundaries, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to accounting services analytics, metric-definition logic, close-quality reporting, reconciliation and exception trends, caveat design, workpaper support, confidentiality handling, and signoff-safe escalation rather than generic BI work.

Authority sources cover AICPA CAS and SSARS clarification, AICPA quality management, AICPA professional conduct, PCAOB audit documentation and evidence standards, SEC KPI guidance, APQC process framework, IRS taxpayer-data safeguards, and FTC customer-information safeguards.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope analysis, metric ambiguity, stale or incomplete data, conflicting published KPIs, unsupported causal and policy claims, unsupported client-facing approval, workpaper-supported exception trends, confidentiality restrictions, advisory and attestation boundaries, terminology normalization, missing facts, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/accounting-tax-and-audit-services/accounting-services-analytics-specialist/ so it remains distinct from adjacent bookkeeping, client accounting services, compliance reporting, audit engagement, tax, legal, editorial, QA, delivery management, and account lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated accounting services analytics specialist for close-quality analytics, reconciliation and exception reporting, source reconciliation, caveat design, confidentiality-aware delivery, and safe escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
